subject: Herald daemon — the process that bridges intra-agent messages to live Claude Code sessions
title: Herald Daemon — agent_messages Poller and Headless CC Spawner
what: Defines the Herald daemon: a long-running (or cron-triggered) process that polls the agent_messages Supabase table for pending messages addressed to this spoke, spawns a headless Claude Code session in the correct spoke directory to respond, waits for it to exit, and marks the message row as responded. Herald is the mechanism that gives agent_messages table its value — without Herald, messages sit unanswered. Herald also handles lifecycle: spawned sessions are short-lived (respond and exit), TTL-expired messages are marked expired and not spawned, and Herald writes a structured spawned_by=herald flag into session-guard.json before each spawn.
why: The agent_messages table (spec-intra-agent-comms-v1) is inert without a polling mechanism. Herald is that mechanism. It must run in the target spoke's environment (not the sender's) because the responding session needs access to that spoke's files, WAI-State, and tooling. Herald cannot run as part of a normal user session — it must be an independent background process so it can respond to messages that arrive while the user session is doing other work. The spawned_by=herald flag is critical: without it, every Herald-spawned session would enter full wakeup and potentially claim chains, burning tokens and causing unintended side effects.
audience: Infrastructure/ops — runs and monitors Herald processes per spoke
Session startup — reads spawned_by=herald flag to configure auto-exit behavior
spec-intra-agent-comms-v1 implementers — Herald is the runtime for that spec's communication patterns
Ozi — relies on Herald being operational for consult-before-deliver and handoff patterns
use_cases: Consult response: session A sends consult to spoke B; Herald in spoke B detects it, spawns headless CC, CC reads the question and responds, Herald marks responded
Handoff receipt: session A sends handoff message to spoke B with chain context; Herald spawns CC in spoke B; CC reads handoff, claims the chain, sends ack
Multi-spoke coordination: Hub session asks 3 spokes a question simultaneously; 3 Herald instances each spawn a responder; all respond within minutes
Herald health monitoring: Sentinel tracks Herald last_poll_at and poll_success_rate; alerts when Herald has not polled for 2x its configured interval
patterns: Poll-spawn-wait-mark cycle: Herald polls → finds pending message → spawns CC → waits for CC exit → marks responded/failed
spawned_by=herald flag: written to WAI-Spoke/runtime/session-guard.json BEFORE spawning CC; session startup reads it and sets auto_exit_after_response=true
One spawn per message: Herald does not batch-spawn; each pending message gets its own CC session in sequence (or in parallel up to max_concurrent_spawns)
TTL check before spawn: if message expires_at < now(), mark expired, do not spawn — avoids wasting a CC session on a stale request

Idempotent poll: if Herald crashes mid-spawn, the message remains status=pending; on next poll Herald will re-spawn (the responder session must handle duplicate spawn gracefully by checking thread for existing response)

herald_config.spoke_directory: string — absolute path to spoke root for CC spawn

herald_poll_loop_pseudocode: pending = query agent_messages WHERE to_spoke=wheel_id AND status='pending' AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())
expired = query agent_messages WHERE to_spoke=wheel_id AND status='pending' AND expires_at <= now()
mark expired rows: UPDATE agent_messages SET status='expired' WHERE id IN expired.ids
for each msg in pending (up to max_concurrent_spawns):
write session-guard.json herald block with msg.thread_id, msg.id
if CC exited 0 and response row present: mark msg status='responded'
else: increment failure count, leave status='pending' for retry

constraints: Herald MUST write spawned_by=herald to session-guard.json BEFORE spawning CC — if this step fails, do not spawn
Herald MUST check TTL before spawning — never spawn a CC session for an expired message
Herald-spawned CC sessions MUST be headless — no interactive terminal; use claude --headless or equivalent flag
A Herald-spawned session that fails to write a response within 10 minutes is considered failed; Herald marks message status=pending for retry
Herald must not run inside a normal user session — it is a separate process; do not invoke Herald's poll loop from session startup
max_concurrent_spawns should be 1 for spokes with limited resources; higher values require sufficient CPU and memory
tests: Basic poll-spawn-respond: write pending message to agent_messages for this spoke; trigger Herald poll; verify CC spawned, response written, message status=responded
TTL expiry: write message with expires_at = 1 second ago; trigger Herald poll; verify message marked expired, no CC spawned
Herald flag check: after Herald spawns CC, verify WAI-Spoke/runtime/session-guard.json contains spawned_by=herald and correct message_id
Auto-exit: Herald-spawned CC session writes response and exits with code 0; verify it does not proceed to wakeup banner or chain claiming
Crash recovery: write pending message; simulate Herald crash mid-spawn; restart Herald; verify message still pending; verify re-spawn succeeds
Sentinel monitoring: Sentinel reads herald.json last_poll_at; verify alert fires when last_poll_at > 2 * poll_interval_seconds ago

open_questions: Deployment mode: cron (simpler, 30s latency per message) vs always-on daemon (lower latency, needs process supervision). For MVP, cron is sufficient.
How does Herald run headless CC in WSL2 without a TTY? Requires testing with claude --print or equivalent non-interactive mode.
Should Herald be per-spoke (each spoke runs its own Herald) or hub-hosted (Hub Herald routes to target spoke directories)? Per-spoke is simpler and more sovereign.

perceive: Read this spec. Read spec-intra-agent-comms-v1 for agent_messages table schema. Check if WAI-Spoke/advisors/herald/ directory exists. Read WAI-Spoke/runtime/session-guard.json for current structure to understand how to add spawned_by block.
execute: 1. Create WAI-Spoke/advisors/herald/herald.json config scaffold. 2. Implement herald_poll.py: query agent_messages, TTL check, write session-guard herald block, spawn CC headless, wait, mark responded. 3. Add spawned_by=herald detection to session startup (wai-enter.sh): if present, set AUTO_EXIT=true, skip full wakeup. 4. Wire herald_poll.py as a cron job (initial deployment). 5. Add Herald health metric to Sentinel watch list.
verify: 1. herald.json config exists and parses. 2. Write test message to agent_messages; run herald_poll.py manually; verify CC spawned. 3. session-guard.json has spawned_by=herald block before CC launch. 4. Herald-spawned session exits after responding without full wakeup. 5. Expired message not spawned.
